retrieved.title: Degree Programmes offered at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
The University of Vienna is one of the most prestiguous and largest universities in Europe and offers a wide range of degree programmes. From education, social sciences, philosophy and religion, history, society, politics and arts over culture, media, mathematics, informatics and technology, natural and life sciences, psychology, health and sports tp languages, economic sciences and law.

This diversity gives students the opportunity to choose an individualised way through their studies that best suits their interests and abilities.

- Bachelor programme

  The bachelor programmes offered at the University of Vienna comprise 180 ECTS and are designed for a standard duration of three years (exception: Teacher Education Programme of 240 ECTS with a standard duration of four years). The language of instruction of all bachelor programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Additionally, English knowledge on level B2 is required for the application for admision for the bachelor programme International Law.
  Graduates of an eligible bachelor programme can apply for admission to a master programme.

  Related Links:

  - [Range of studies: bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/)

retrieved.text:
Here you find a list of all bachelor and diploma programmes offered at the University of Vienna. [You may also browse the programmes by topic.](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

German knowledge on C1 level is an admission requirement for all bachelor/diploma programmes. The language(s) of instruction of each course depend(s) on the focus of the degree programme.

Please note that the language of instruction of most of these programmes is German only. To start the degree programme, you need to hold a [certificate of German proficiency on C1 level](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

[Information about the teacher education programme (bachelor and master) can be found here.](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

Information about the admission procedure can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance exam procedure.

heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
retrieved.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. retrieved.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
retrieved.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science-bachelor-with-aptitute-test/) | [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |

The language of instruction of these programmes is German and English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |

retrieved.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: I want to study a bachelor/diploma programme
General requirements for the application for admission to bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
- scan of your valid your passport or personal ID card (front and back!) as JPEG or PNG file
- all pages (!) of your original secondary-school leaving certificate
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- [list of subjects](/en/admission/bachelordiploma-programmes/list-of-subjects/) in its original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - together with a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- if you have already studied: transcripts/diplomas in their original version
  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
  - plus sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)
- for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- for the bachelor programmes in International Legal Studies and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science: [proof of English language proficiency](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/) at level B2 or higher

You have to submit the application for admission within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The application period depends on your citizenship. Please note: [degree programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) have a special application period.

retrieved.title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
retrieved.text:
Studying at the University of Vienna means being part of a large and diverse community. Students from around 140 different countries are currently pursuing a degree at the University of Vienna. Our participation in networks such as Erasmus+ and partnerships with the best universities in the world open up numerous exciting exchange opportunities. Diversity is encouraged by applying the principle of equal opportunities in all areas of university activity.

- [#univie communities](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/univie-communities/#c487599)
- [Summer & Winter Schools](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/univie-summer/winter-schools)
- [Student Mobility](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Diversity portal of the University of Vienna](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/diversity)
- [Accessible studying at the University of Vienna](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/) (in German)

© University of Vienna / Barbara Mair

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

The University of Vienna is also the largest research and educational institution in Austria with a total of 7,400 academics. Our teaching staff adheres to the principle of research-led teaching in their courses. They impart knowledge about latest research findings and their students are able to participate in original research. The University of Vienna sets future-oriented accents in interdisciplinary innovative research fields.

- [Financial support by the University of Vienna](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/stipendien/) (in German)
- [Research Services and Career Development](https://forschung.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [The University of Vienna´s strategic priorities](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/research)

retrieved.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
retrieved.text:
Find out in advance which requirements you must meet with documents from your country in order to fulfil the admission requirements at the University of Vienna. The competence check only makes sense if you meet all the requirements.

Prospective students who do not fulfil all requirements cannot be admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

Also check the specific requirements for your desired degree programme, such as proof of German and/or English language proficiency and further required documents. You can find information on this on the [page for the respective degree programme](/en/admission-procedure/).

Please also ensure that your documents are correctly legalised and translated.

- [Documents from Bangladesh](#c1270047)
- [Documents from India](#c1270039)
- [Documents from Pakistan](#c1270031)

Your documents have to be legalised by [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121827) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

retrieved.text:
Asylum seekers, convention refugees, and people granted subsidiary protection who possess a secondary-school leaving certificate and/or a university degree from a non-EU/EEA country may apply for admission to a degree programme at the University of Vienna. The requirements are as follows: you must fulfil the admission requirements and adhere to the [application and admission period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/).

The University of Vienna offers over 150 bachelor, master and doctoral programmes. You can get more detailed information about the programmes offered on the following pages:

retrieved.title: Services for graduates
retrieved.text:
#### Becoming a mentor

No matter if you would like to share your experiences on finding a job, staying abroad or establishing companies and enterprises — on the alma Mentoring Platform of the University of Vienna you can [**become a mentor**](https://mentoring.univie.ac.at/en/page/MentorInnen) and share your knowledge with other graduates.

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

**Continuing to seek knowledge.**Graduating from the University of Vienna does not mean that you can no longer access the University’s information resources. Graduates can continue to use their library accounts or extend them (€ 15 per year) and continue to use electronic resources free of charge (at the locations of the Vienna University Library and via the internal university Wi-Fi).

***Benefits for alumni:****Members of the Alumni Association receive a 50 % discount on library cards (€ 7.50).*

- [Vienna University Library](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/en/)

© USI Wien / Barbara Mair

**Staying fit.**From Aikido to Zumba — the University Sport Institute (USI) offers courses for around 160 different types of sports as well as continuing education and training programmes.

***Benefits for alumni:****All USI courses are available to university graduates at special alumni rates.*

- [University Sport Institute of the University of Vienna](https://musi.usi.at/?lang=en)

© Universität Wien / Elia Zilberberg

retrieved.title: Choosing the right programme
retrieved.text:
Studienplattform (Study Platform) is an important tool for your independent research and offers up-to-date information and useful links to all fields of degree Programmes in Austria.

On this website, which is provided by OeAD (Östereichischer Austauschdienst), you can find a lot of valuable information about studying in Austria.

Watch the film ["Study in Austria"](https://youtu.be/IcBJh8AyHC8)  to find out why Austria is the ideal place to study.

- Bachelor programme

  The bachelor programmes offered at the University of Vienna comprise 180 ECTS and are designed for a standard duration of three years (exception: Teacher Education Programme of 240 ECTS with a standard duration of four years). The language of instruction of all bachelor programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Additionally, English knowledge on level B2 is required for the application for admision for the bachelor programme International Law.
  Graduates of an eligible bachelor programme can apply for admission to a master programme.

reranked.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
reranked.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. The entrance

reranked.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
reranked.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

General requirements for the application for admission to bachelor/diploma programmes
reranked.text:
- scan of your valid your passport or personal ID card (front and back!) as JPEG or PNG file

  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country

  - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country

You have to submit the application for admission within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The application period depends on your citizenship. reranked.title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
reranked.text:
Studying at the University of Vienna means being part of a large and diverse community. Students from around 140 different countries are currently pursuing a degree at the University of Vienna. Our participation in networks such as Erasmus+ and partnerships with the best universities in the world open up numerous exciting exchange opportunities. Diversity is encouraged by applying the principle of equal opportunities in all areas of university activity.

- [#univie communities](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/univie-communities/#c487599)
- [Summer & Winter Schools](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/univie-summer/winter-schools)
- [Student Mobility](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Diversity portal of the University of Vienna](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/diversity)
- [Accessible studying at the University of Vienna](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/) (in German)

© University of Vienna / Barbara Mair

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

The University of Vienna is also the largest research and educational institution in Austria with a total of 7,400 academics. Our teaching staff adheres to the principle of research-led teaching in their courses. They impart knowledge about latest research findings and their students are able to participate in original research. The University of Vienna sets future-oriented accents in interdisciplinary innovative research fields.

- [Financial support by the University of Vienna](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/stipendien/) (in German)
- [Research Services and Career Development](https://forschung.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [The University of Vienna´s strategic priorities](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/research)

reranked.title: Competence check at VFS Global
heading_path: Step 1: Inform yourself about the admission requirements
reranked.text:
Find out in advance which requirements you must meet with documents from your country in order to fulfil the admission requirements at the University of Vienna. The competence check only makes sense if you meet all the requirements.

Prospective students who do not fulfil all requirements cannot be admitted to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

Also check the specific requirements for your desired degree programme, such as proof of German and/or English language proficiency and further required documents. You can find information on this on the [page for the respective degree programme](/en/admission-procedure/).

Please also ensure that your documents are correctly legalised and translated.

- [Documents from Bangladesh](#c1270047)
- [Documents from India](#c1270039)
- [Documents from Pakistan](#c1270031)

Your documents have to be legalised by [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/#c1121827) and must be translated into either German or English afterwards.

Your steps in the admission procedure depend on the degree programme of your choice.

reranked.text:
#### Becoming a mentor

No matter if you would like to share your experiences on finding a job, staying abroad or establishing companies and enterprises — on the alma Mentoring Platform of the University of Vienna you can [**become a mentor**](https://mentoring.univie.ac.at/en/page/MentorInnen) and share your knowledge with other graduates.

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

**Continuing to seek knowledge.**Graduating from the University of Vienna does not mean that you can no longer access the University’s information resources. Graduates can continue to use their library accounts or extend them (€ 15 per year) and continue to use electronic resources free of charge (at the locations of the Vienna University Library and via the internal university Wi-Fi).

***Benefits for alumni:****Members of the Alumni Association receive a 50 % discount on library cards (€ 7.50).*

- [Vienna University Library](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/en/)

© USI Wien / Barbara Mair

**Staying fit.**From Aikido to Zumba — the University Sport Institute (USI) offers courses for around 160 different types of sports as well as continuing education and training programmes.

***Benefits for alumni:****All USI courses are available to university graduates at special alumni rates.*

reranked.title: Choosing the right programme
reranked.text:
Studienplattform (Study Platform) is an important tool for your independent research and offers up-to-date information and useful links to all fields of degree Programmes in Austria.

On this website, which is provided by OeAD (Östereichischer Austauschdienst), you can find a lot of valuable information about studying in Austria.

Watch the film ["Study in Austria"](https://youtu.be/IcBJh8AyHC8)  to find out why Austria is the ideal place to study.

© Universität Wien/ Barbara Mair

BerufsInfoZentrum (BIZ - Job Information Centre) is your first contact point for questions on professions, trainings and job opportunities.

© Universität Wien/ Barbara Mair

"Get your own idea of your profession": With this slogan the Berufsinformationszentrum der Wiener Wirtschaft (BiWi - Vocational Information Centre of the Vienna Economic Chamber) provides orientation for your career and educational choice.

© Universität Wien/ Barbara Mair

Bildungsberatung in Wien (Educational Counselling in Vienna) offers free and neutral educational and career counselling to Viennese residents.

selected.title: Degree Programmes offered at the University of Vienna
selected.text:
The University of Vienna is one of the most prestiguous and largest universities in Europe and offers a wide range of degree programmes. From education, social sciences, philosophy and religion, history, society, politics and arts over culture, media, mathematics, informatics and technology, natural and life sciences, psychology, health and sports tp languages, economic sciences and law.

This diversity gives students the opportunity to choose an individualised way through their studies that best suits their interests and abilities.

heading_path: B
selected.text:
/

- Bachelor programme

  The bachelor programmes offered at the University of Vienna comprise 180 ECTS and are designed for a standard duration of three years (exception: Teacher Education Programme of 240 ECTS with a standard duration of four years). The language of instruction of all bachelor programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Additionally, English knowledge on level B2 is required for the application for admision for the bachelor programme International Law.
  Graduates of an eligible bachelor programme can apply for admission to a master programme.

selected.text:
Here you find a list of all bachelor and diploma programmes offered at the University of Vienna. [You may also browse the programmes by topic.](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

German knowledge on C1 level is an admission requirement for all bachelor/diploma programmes. The language(s) of instruction of each course depend(s) on the focus of the degree programme.

Please note that the language of instruction of most of these programmes is German only. To start the degree programme, you need to hold a [certificate of German proficiency on C1 level](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

[Information about the teacher education programme (bachelor and master) can be found here.](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance exam procedure.

selected.title: Information for asylum seekers
heading_path: How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
selected.text: How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring. You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

[3] Bachelor/diploma programmes
Here you find a list of all bachelor and diploma programmes offered at the University of Vienna. [You may also browse the programmes by topic.](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/bachelor-programmes-by-topic/)

German knowledge on C1 level is an admission requirement for all bachelor/diploma programmes. The language(s) of instruction of each course depend(s) on the focus of the degree programme.

Please note that the language of instruction of most of these programmes is German only. To start the degree programme, you need to hold a [certificate of German proficiency on C1 level](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

[Information about the teacher education programme (bachelor and master) can be found here.](/en/degree-programmes/teacher-education-programme/)

Information about the admission procedure can be found here.

For some degree programmes you need to pass an entrance exam procedure.

[6] Information for asylum seekers — How do I get admitted to a degree programme?
How do I get admitted to a degree programme? In order to be admitted to a bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna you are required to possess a secondary-school leaving certificate or to have completed a degree programme of at least three years. Admission to master programmes require the completion of a subject-related bachelor programme. - You need to provide the required documents in their original or legalised form. If you no longer possess your secondary-school leaving certificate or your higher education degree certificate, you may request a substitute confirmation at the [Federal Ministry Women, Science and Research Republic of Austria](https://www.bmfwf.gv.at/public.html). Admission is not possible without a secondary-school leaving certificate or higher education degree certificate. - Documents issued in a language other than German or English additionally require a legally attested translation. Your documents do not require [full diplomatic certification](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/), irrespective of the issuing country. However, you need to prove your status as a recognised refugee/asylum seeker (e.g. by means of a convention travel document). You can submit your application for admission online on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/). This is only possible during the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/). The University of Vienna conducts an [entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/) for select bachelor/diploma programmes. An entrance exam procedure is also conducted for admission to the bachelor programme in Teacher Education. You can apply for admission to these programmes only once per academic year, in spring.
